issues.title: [Proposal] Multiline TextField should support both submit and enter a newline
comments: Hi @MilesAdamson, Thanks for filing the issue, I can confirm with multiline Textfield we cannot both submit and enter a new line. Labeling this issue as a feature request.

As an example of this issue, we currently use `Overlay` to show a "done" button to iOS users since they cannot reliably close their own keyboards with native buttons. This causes a different issue where flutter scrolls to the text field you are typing in, but the bar covers it unless you manually scroll further

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29047682/158474904-e1c21cea-7f5c-4016-b487-08c72cd6d6cc.mp4
Any updates? I don't want to add a buttons/gestures to allow iOS users to close their keyboard everywhere

> Any updates? I don't want to add a buttons/gestures to allow iOS users to close their keyboard everywhere

i think firstly you have to set resizeToAvoidBottomInset: true, and after that you can add a FocusNode to the TextField. place the focus node in your widget's state. In your build function add the focus when creating the TextField. you can also refer
[this](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/65224279/flutter-textfield-how-to-support-submission-on-enter-and-newline-on-shift)

That makes it so that shift+enter creates a newline, and enter closes the keyboard. No one on mobile is going to expect to tap the shift key then enter to add a newline.

Really that is just a workaround for the same core problem that users cannot close their own keyboard with multiline, and is much better suited for websites where shift+enter is a known UX
I wonder how this works on native iOS and Android?  Can you create a multiline field with both enter and submit buttons, and if so, how?
It has been 3 years already...

issues.title: Is that possible to replace the keyboard position with another widget and keep TextField position unchanged during keyboard animation?
issues.body:
Flutter version

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel master, v1.5.9-pre.205, on Mac OS X 10.13.6 17G4015, locale en-CN)
```

Hi, I will try my best to describe the problem. Basically, I want to achieve what many messenger apps do when clicking Emoji icon when keyboard is present. All messenger apps including Facebook messenger, WeChat etc.. are doing the same thing. But I have spent two days to try to do the same in Flutter, but failed. 

First, hiding and showing keyboard are easy by the code `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide');` and `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.show');`

When clicking the Emoji button, I want the textfield row position remains unchanged, then hide the keyboard and the keyboard original position should be replaced by the emoji panel. But the current behavior is the keyboard showing and hiding actions will change the UI layout during the animation. This does not happen to all the native apps that I use. Something like the following:

## Showing Emoji Panel When Keyboard OnScreen

Original Screen:

```
-------Column-------
-- ListView
----------------------
-- TextFieldRow  
----------------------
-- Keyboard
----------------------
```

After clicking Emoji Icon:

Phase 1 Screen:

```
-------Column-------
-- ListView
----------------------
-- TextFieldRow  
----------------------
-- Emoji Panel
----------------------
-- Keyboard
----------------------
```

ListView and Emoji Panel positions changed because Both Emoji Panel and Keyboard are present at the same time. and NOTE THAT their positions will keep changing during keyboard moving down.

Phase 2 Screen:

```
-------Column-------
-- ListView
----------------------
-- TextFieldRow  
----------------------
-- Emoji Panel
----------------------
```

ListView and Emoji Panel positions changed because Keyboard is finally gone.

## Hiding Emoji Panel and Bring Keyboard Back

Original Screen:

```
-------Column-------
-- ListView
----------------------
-- TextFieldRow  
----------------------
-- Emoji Panel
----------------------
```

Clicking Emoji Icon (or Keyboard Icon)

Phase 1 Screen:

```
-------Column-------
-- ListView
----------------------
-- TextFieldRow  
----------------------
```

ListView and TextFieldRow positions changed because emoji panel is gone. 

Phase 2 Screen:

```
-------Column-------
-- ListView
----------------------
-- TextFieldRow  
----------------------
-- Keyboard
----------------------
```

ListView and Emoji Panel's positions keeps changing while keyboard is moving up. 

## What I expect

I expect the ListView and TextFieldRow will not change their positions while the keyboard is moving up or down and emoji shows or hides. And this is what all native apps I used so far do. I tried many codes including playing around Stack, resizeToAvoidBottomPadding property etc.. But nothing works so far. 

(Btw, I can get keyboard height with `MediaQuery.of(context).viewInsets.bottom`, so setting the same height to emoji panel as the keyboard is not a problem. and even if the emoji panel has a a little bit different height, the positions change should be around some pixels instead of the current behaviors)

Thank you very much for your help.

issues.title: [Windows] Text selection in a input lost on right click
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Wrap the TextField with SelectionArea.
2. Select the input in the TextField.
3. Right click on the selection text, and then open menu, it will lose selection.

### Expected results

Select input in the TextField , and then right click open the menu, will not lose selection.

Right click open the menu, it will lose selection.

issues.title: RTL TextField breaks when inserting emojis between existing emojis
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

When using a TextField with RTL text direction, inserting an emoji between two existing emojis sometimes breaks the text, showing ? character.

### Expected results

Inserting emojis between existing emojis in an RTL TextField should render correctly.

### Actual results

The inserted emoji breaks the text and may render as ?.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
TextField(textDirection: TextDirection.rtl)
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/706052f5-2309-4bdf-a44b-6ab05848f8a7

</details>


### Logs

<details colose><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
n/a
```

</details>


### Flutter Doctor output

<details colose><summary>Doctor output</summary>

```console
n/a
```

</details>
comments:
The issue behaves differently across platforms.

On Flutter Desktop (Windows/Linux/macOS), dynamically changing textDirection on an active TextField can crash the Flutter Engine. During the rebuild of the native text input layout, the engine attempts to convert a wide string (`std::wstring`) to UTF-8 using `wstring_convert::to_bytes`.
If the text is in an intermediate or invalid Unicode state (for example, during IME composition or layout recalculation), this conversion fails and throws a std::range_error with the message:
```
wstring_convert: to_bytes error
```


Since the Flutter Engine is built with -fno-exceptions, the exception cannot be caught, resulting in an application abort.

On mobile platforms (Android/iOS), the app usually does not crash because a different text input pipeline is used. However, changing textDirection during active text composition may invalidate the IME composing state, causing unsupported or incomplete Unicode sequences to be replaced with a fallback character (**displayed as ? or �**).
@puneetkukreja98 this is a problem in macos as well.

@warioddly I’m able to reproduce this issue on macOS as well. Thanks for reporting it.

issues.title: [VoiceOver] Can not focus on TextField when inside sliver list that is scrolled.
issues.body:
Internal: b/151285964

<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill our the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

When a text field is in the body of a sliver list, and you scroll the sliver list under a sliver app bar, the text field is no longer editable through iOS VoiceOver.

Seems similar to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/45394.

## Steps to Reproduce

Using the following example app: 

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(Demo());
}

class Demo extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: CustomScrollView(
          slivers: [
            SliverAppBar(
              pinned: true,
              expandedHeight: 200,
              flexibleSpace: Container(height: 100),
            ),
            SliverList(
              delegate: SliverChildListDelegate([
                Container(
                  height: 400,
                  child: Center(child: TextField()),
                ),
                Container(
                  height: 400,
                  child: Center(child: Text('empty box')),
                ),
              ]),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run the above app on an iOS device. 
1. Enable VoiceOver mode. 
1. Scroll the app down so that the `SliverAppBar` is collapsed. 
1. Attempt to edit the text field, notice that you can not focus on the text field, despite the VoiceOver saying "Text field, double tap to edit".

Also notice that if the app is _not_ scrolled, the `TextField` is editable even with VoiceOver enabled. 

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
Text field can be focused on and text can be entered.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
Text field can not be focused on or edited. 

<details>
  <summary>Flutter doctor output</summary>

<!--
      Run your application with `flutter run --verbose` and attach all the
      log output below between the lines with the backticks. If there is an

-->

I'm using the [iPhone XS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IPhone_XS), _not_ the XS Max, but rather the smaller size (which could account for the difference). 

And by "cannot focus on the text field", I mean when I navigate to the text field with voiceover, it says "Text Field, double tap to enter", but when I double tap, it does not bring the keyboard up. 

Can you try it again on your device but make the second container ~`1200` pixels tall instead of `400`? I think it's important that the header fully collapse before the bug reproduces.

There's still an issue here: If you have an accessibility node with a text field inside you cannot input-focus it if the textfield is not in the center of the node, see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/52863#issuecomment-604159185.

Thanks for the stack trace, I tried again just now but was still unable to get it to give me one. Just to give my thoughts this is likely due to executing some Scribble codepaths when we don't need to because it's disabled, and/or some directly flawed logic leading to bad indices.

I haven't had time to debug this, but from briefly looking at it, `[FlutterTextInputPlugin closestPositionToPoint:withinRange:]` assumes that `_selectionRects` is always non empty when it is called. So either that assumption is wrong, or `_selectionRects` is empty when it shouldn't be. Maybe that rings some bells to someone?

@knopp 
Set `stylusHandwritingEnabled`  false  will case   `_selectionRects`  not set,

issues.title: [iOS] Chinese Pinyin Input Method Leaves Invisible Characters When Focus Changes Between TextFields
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Set up iOS device with Chinese Pinyin input method
2. Create a Flutter app with two TextField widgets
3. Focus on the first TextField
4. Type Chinese pinyin letters "nihaoma" using the system keyboard (do NOT tap the confirm/space key)
5. Directly tap on the second TextField to switch focus (without confirming the pinyin input)
6. Observe the content in the first TextField

### Expected results

Behavior should match iOS native UITextField:

1. When focus is lost during pinyin input, the TextField should automatically clean up composing state
2. The TextField should contain clean text "nihaoma" (without invisible separator characters)
3. This matches how iOS native input fields handle the same scenario

### Actual results

Flutter TextField retains invisible characters:

1. The first TextField displays "ni hao ma" with invisible six-per-em space characters (U+2006)
2. These invisible separator characters are inserted by iOS input method during pinyin composition
3. The characters can be detected using tools like https://invisiblecharacterviewer.com/

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da6f5011-9dc3-4110-b367-8ea31374650c)

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(MyApp());

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Chinese Input Test')),
        body: Padding(
          padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
          child: Column(
            children: [
              TextField(
                decoration: InputDecoration(

                  labelText: 'TextField 2 - Tap here to switch focus',
                  border: OutlineInputBorder(),
                ),

            ],

    );
  }
}
```

issues.title: Support keyboard events in flutter_driver
issues.body:
Add an API for sending keyboard events to devices in Flutter Driver tests.

Useful for writing comprehensive tests of text input, input from physical keyboards, IME compose region etc.
comments: /cc @yjbanov @jason-simmons
This would also be helpful to enable keyboard shortcuts to navigate the widget tree when performing object inspection.
Any updates on this?
No. Also, ideas are welcome for how to implement this, especially in a way that doesn't tie the driver API to any specific platform.
Closing as dupe of #6737.
Reopening this one as it's more accurate than #6737
Is there any status on this? I'm looking for a way to dismiss the keyboard after setting some input.

There has been no progress on this. We do not yet have a way to expose this via `FlutterDriver` interface in a platform-agnostic way. However, you may be able to do this by calling `adb` directly. I have not tried it myself, but I found this: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/32396227/hide-soft-keyboard-using-adb
Great, what about iOS?
Maybe this: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/18755410/how-to-dismiss-keyboard-ios-programmatically-when-pressing-return?

Maybe I'm misunderstanding something, but couldn't the API for `FlutterDriver` look similar to [the one for flutter test](https://docs.flutter.io/flutter/flutter_test/TestTextInput/receiveAction.html)?

@kentcb `FlutterDriver` is meant to do end-to-end testing where things are as real as possible. `receiveAction` fakes text entry. If you are using this method, you probably don't want to write a driver test, but a plain widget test, which would also be 30x faster to run. For the simple case when you just need to enter some text we have [enterText](https://docs.flutter.io/flutter/flutter_driver/FlutterDriver/enterText.html).

@yjbanov I think you may have missed my point. I'm aware of the purpose of `FlutterDriver`, and am using it myself to capture screenshots for the app store submissions. However, I cannot create certain screenshots because doing so requires that I press "enter" on the keyboard. I am already using `FlutterDriver.enterText` to send text to a control, but in one case I need to press "enter" to actuate the entered text.

You asked [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/9383#issuecomment-329531814) of a way to implement this without tying to a platform. My question was why can't `FlutterDriver` have a similar API to `TestTextInput`. Something like `FlutterDriver.receiveAction(TextInputAction)`. Sure, the input actions are not always applicable to every platform, but that didn't stop the API being implemented for widget tests, so I'm not sure why it's blocking a `FlutterDriver` implementation.
@kentcb Could [requestData](https://docs.flutter.io/flutter/flutter_driver/FlutterDriver/requestData.html) help? You should be able to use `TestTextInput` in the `DataHandler`.

@yjbanov Possibly, though I can't figure out how to get a `TestTextInput` (and therefore a `WidgetTester`) in the context of an `enableFlutterDriverExtension` handler. Which is kind of the point: it shouldn't be this hard.

In the case of `requestData` you wouldn't be using a `WidgetTester` (although @collinjackson is working on adding this capability). To get a `TestTextInput`, I think you should be able to `new TestTextInput()` and use that.
@yjbanov  any progress on this?
@martipello  Unfortunately, no.

Nearly 3 years later I go hunting for a way to get flutter_driver to send a keyboard.next type event, and find this.

Feels like a meme. At this rate I'll have to just add a button to press.

@flukejones are you me? I'm here for the same reason and thinking on the same meme-solution... I'm starting to think Google doesn't care enough about this project, there are so many important features missing... I find myself all time looking for workarounds for testing my flutter app.
Having same problem, without submit button there is no way to start search after entering text in field.
What's the update on this issue?
any update on this issue ?
any update?
Quite a few things that I can't write integration tests for without this feature. Can't test the flow of Forms and all our search fields, as they all need a keyboard submit.

Having same problem. It would be great if there would be an option to disable `_testTextInput.register();` in `FlutterDriverExtension`. In tests side it would cause no harm to simply add `await driver.setTextEntryEmulation(enabled: true);` explicitly. It would unblock manual testing with automation builds.
any update? is there any solution to generate keyboard events in flutter_driver tests?

> any update? is there any solution to generate keyboard events in flutter_driver tests?

@asifnawaz2311 use integration_test, new standard for E2E tests in Flutter. They support keyboard events, for example submit as `await tester.testTextInput.receiveAction(TextInputAction.done);`

> @asifnawaz2311 use integration_test, new standard for E2E tests in Flutter. They support keyboard events, for example submit as `await tester.testTextInput.receiveAction(TextInputAction.done);`

@vrnvorona  how to get a TestTextInput (and therefore a WidgetTester) in the context of an enableFlutterDriverExtension handler

> > @asifnawaz2311 use integration_test, new standard for E2E tests in Flutter. They support keyboard events, for example submit as `await tester.testTextInput.receiveAction(TextInputAction.done);`
> 
> @vrnvorona how to get a TestTextInput (and therefore a WidgetTester) in the context of an enableFlutterDriverExtension handler

Please read official documentation for this package https://flutter.dev/docs/testing/integration-tests. But keep in mind that `IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized();` should be outside of setUpAll block, otherwise it won't work.

In short: we don't have enableFlutterDriverExtension anymore. Tests are now written as Widget Tests, but run through driver.

> > any update? is there any solution to generate keyboard events in flutter_driver tests?
> 
> @asifnawaz2311 use integration_test, new standard for E2E tests in Flutter. They support keyboard events, for example submit as `await tester.testTextInput.receiveAction(TextInputAction.done);`

integration_test doesn't support running in profile mode, therefore for those of us wishing to run tests requiring keyboard events, and in as a means of testing performance, this doesn't work.

Hi Flutter team, 

I know you're pushing the integration_test package for a solution to this, but in some cases it won't work, although it might be a very small number of cases. 

I'm the creator of [SessionMate](https://sessionmate.dev/) a tool for Flutter developers to replay bugs their users experience in production. I use Flutter driver as my underlying user interaction replay technology. 

I would like to get a few minutes of one of the driver maintainers' time on a call. If they can walk me through the code and how things fit together I will look for a solution to this issue, or maintain my own fork of it with a fix, since my business will depend on it. 

Until then I will leave it as not supported and use user-supplied code as a workaround. 

Please feel free to contact me at dane@filledstacks.com

issues.title: Gboard Keyboard language change is not working when `enableSuggestions` is false
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

Samsung Galaxy, Xiaomi, etc. GBoard keyboard language change not working.

1. Put focus on text field.
2. Click on language change button on bottom GBoard keyboard.
3. Not working.

- Setting the `enableSuggestions` option to `false` doesn't seem to work.
- We have verified that third-party keyboards work even when the `enableSuggestions` value is `false`.

### Expected results

The Keyboard language should be changed normally.

### Actual results

The Keyboard language change not working.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(
      home: MyHomePage(),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key});

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(

}
```

issues.title: EditableText's clipBehaviour only clips when overflowing horizontally
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce 1. Create an `EditableText` with a `TextStyle` that has a reduced height (0.5). 2. Enter any text into the EditableText. It is rendered without clipping. 3. Continue entering text until the EditableText becomes scrollable. Text starts clipping. ### Expected results EditableText should clip it's content even if it is only overflowing vertically. ### Actual results EditableText clips the content only when it overflows horizontally (becomes scrollable). Reproducible on stable releases 3.19.3, 3.22.3, 3.24.0 and on latest master. ### Code sample
Code sample ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(const MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( title: 'Flutter Demo', theme: ThemeData( colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple), useMaterial3: true, ), home: const Scaffold( body: Center( child: Input(), ), ), ); } } class Input extends StatefulWidget { const Input({super.key}); @override State createState() => _InputState(); } class _InputState extends State { final focusNode = FocusNode(); final controller = TextEditingController(); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return DecoratedBox( decoration: BoxDecoration( border: Border.all( color: Colors.black, width: 1, ), ), child: EditableText( clipBehavior: Clip.hardEdge, controller: controller, focusNode: focusNode, style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.displayLarge!.copyWith( height: 0.5, ), cursorColor: Colors.black, backgroundCursorColor: Colors.black, ), ); } } ```
### Screenshots or Video
Screenshots / Video demonstration | Text is not clipping | Text is clipping | | --------------- | --------------- | |
### Logs
Logs ```console [Paste your logs here] ```

Doctor output ```console [✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.24.0, on macOS 14.5 23F79 darwin-arm64, locale en-GB) • Flutter version 3.24.0 on channel stable at /Users/alex/workspace/flutter • Upstream repository https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git • Framework revision 80c2e84975 (12 days ago), 2024-07-30 23:06:49 +0700 • Engine revision b8800d88be • Dart version 3.5.0 • DevTools version 2.37.2 [✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 30.0.3) • Android SDK at /Users/alex/Library/Android/sdk • Platform android-34, build-tools 30.0.3 • ANDROID_HOME = /Users/alex/Library/Android/sdk • Java binary at: /Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home/bin/java • Java version OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 17.0.6+0-17.0.6b829.9-10027231) • All Android licenses accepted. Thank you for the report! I can observe this issue as well (verified on Android and macOS app targets).

<details>

```console

    • DevTools version 2.38.0
    • If those were intentional, you can disregard the above warnings; however it is recommended to use "git" directly to perform update
      checks and upgrades.
```
</details>

Isn't It intended behaviour, to not clip vertically if `maxLines = 1`? I always thought that it's special case and maybe `RenderEditable` intentionally reports real height of text, because it's preferred to actually render text. Vertical clipping works great for multi-line (`maxLines > 1` or `null`) and it makes me think that actual bug here is to clip text **vertically** after **horizontal** scroll appears.

> actual bug here is to clip text **vertically** after **horizontal** scroll appears.

That is the observed behaviour, yes. Any way I look at the issue it still feels like something isn't right. I my opinion the vertical and horizontal overflow should in no way be connected, so that the field is clipped even if it fits horizontally.

It only clips if it `_hasVisualOverflow`:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/f825a7563174cdf526dcf657118a1f37883292a4/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L2567-L2570

Which is defined as:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/f825a7563174cdf526dcf657118a1f37883292a4/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L1698

That looks like an oversight to me... The way it works now (suddenly start clipping vertically when there is enough text to scroll horizontally) is definitely not right for anyone. I'm thinking it should clip that vertical overflow seen in the issue description at all times, even when maxLines is 1, because it would be weird to only crop overflow in the x direction?

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/f825a7563174cdf526dcf657118a1f37883292a4/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L2569-L2569

Can the `_hasVisualOverflow` check just be removed or is the performance optimization big enough that we do not want to clip when nothing is overflowing?

@justinmc I am tinkering around this code to try to come up with a fix, but I am not sure if it is worth checking overflow on every layout or maybe we can just always clip the text?

I tried a naive approach to check the font size against the constraints of the textPainter. This seems to work, but I am not sure if this is viable, since the only way to access the fontSize is through `_textPainter.text!.style!.fontSize!;` and the fields are nullable (and I am sure there are cases where this would not work correctly).

 I also think that this goes deeper, since other things like selections are not clipped too which looks odd in my opinion. 
 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b12ba49-3219-4018-bf4f-58cc66ad2acd)

Rendering WidgetSpans with a custom TagsEditingController inside a TextField is also affected by this. I think this is a bug deserves more attention, as no one is working on this for over a half of a year.

issues.title: HID Input not being processed on iOS 13+
comments:
@alexanderdZiiware 
Thanks for detailed report.
When I tried your steps to replicate with bluetooth enabled keyboard and using iOS simulator (14.3), I observed that I was able to type in in active textfield.
What should be the expected behavior ? 
How to verify `HID inputs are always caught by the AppDelegate.` ?

@darshankawar
Thank you for the quick reply.
Our app relies on catching HID input without any TextField selected. 
If you run our example on an older Flutter version (< 2.0.0), you should notice HID input being caught and printed to the console regardless of the TextField being selected. However, on Flutter 2.0, the HID input only gets printed to the console when a TextField is selected.
We've only noticed this on iOS version 13+, lower versions of iOS correctly catch HID input at all times.

@alexanderdZiiware 
Thanks for these details.
We won't be able to switch back to < 2.0 stable version now, as the downgrade now shows 2.0.1, unless we explicitly download the 1.22 version and verify, but it'll be a bit time consuming task.

Is [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/39900) similar issue resemble yours ?

@darshankawar

That issue seems to be similar but also different to ours. We tried their example code along with our changed `AppDelegate.swift` on Flutter 2.0.2, and we didn't get any HID input from the `AppDelegate.swift` when we should have. For us, we don't really care about the text from the HID input going into the TextField, we're just want the HID input to use in the back end of things. We also tried the example from the linked issue on Flutter 1.20.0 by using the git checkout command for that version in the flutter/bin/ directory to get us to that version, and we were able to get HID input on an iOS 14 device.

Thanks @alexanderdZiiware for clarifying.

issues.title: [Android] TextField text selection toolbar not shown on long Press
issues.body:
### **Steps to Reproduce**

1. Have a TextField in a Flutter web app 
2. Open the app with Android web browser (Chrome) 
3. Tap the field and enter text
4. Short press a word to display the handle
5. Long press to copy/cut

**Expected results:**

- On short press, only one handle should be displayed (the light blue native handle)
- On long press, only native handles should be displayed and a toolbox should be displayed for copying/cutting

**Actual results:**

- On short press, the native handle is drawn behind the Flutter handle (there are 2 handles)
- On long press, only Flutter handles are displayed. The copy/cut toolbox is not displayed.
- If i long press on the Flutter's handles, the native handles are displayed and the copy/cut toolbox is displayed.

**Information:**

- The result is the same on Android emulators, on a Google Pixel 6 Pro and on an Xperia XZ1 Compact (Android 9).
- The result is the same on the examples in the documentation (like [https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField-class.html](url))
- If I run the app on an Android device with Flutter run, everything works fine except the issues in #[98265](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98265)
- The result seems to work fine on Safari of iOS simulators (Cupertino handles).

**Code:**

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class TestTextField extends StatelessWidget {
  const TestTextField({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: SafeArea(
          child: TextField(
            minLines: null,
            maxLines: null,
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

issues.title: [android] Animation when opening keyboard is unsynchronized
issues.body:
This issue is the android version of [#105687](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120555) (which is fixed by https://github.com/flutter/engine/commit/a5ff3629dfecd81d7322c40de7edaab24ee3d21f).

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/53510751/3437e46b-b620-420a-af8b-d69b4ad0dcd6

The keyboard animation is jittery most of time. 
Notice that the issue is much evident and occurs often on real device running in release mode.

As pointed out in the iOS fix, issue is:
"if setViewportMetrics called **before** framework's vsync process callback (rebuild & layout & paint), will cause jitter keyboard animation.So this PR is to keep the time when updateViewportMetrics call to framework side legal".

Demo code here:
https://github.com/delfme/flutter-native-text-input-repo

With this fix, flutter would finally provide a native-like keyboard animation on both ios and android 🚀

issues.title: iOS App Hang FlutterPlatformPlugin getClipboardData blocks main thread for 2+ seconds
issues.body:
## Steps to reproduce

1. Enable Universal Clipboard across Apple devices (e.g., iPhone + Mac)
2. Copy content on one device (e.g., Mac)
3. Before clipboard sync completes, tap on a `TextField` in the Flutter app on iPhone
4. App freezes for 2+ seconds

**Note:** This issue is timing-dependent and intermittent, making it difficult to reproduce on demand. However, it occurs regularly in production environments and is captured by crash/ANR monitoring tools (Sentry).

**Affected iOS versions:** iOS 14+ (confirmed on iOS 26.1)
**Affected devices:** Various iPhones (confirmed on iPhone 18,2)

## Analysis

The root cause appears to be in `FlutterPlatformPlugin.mm` line 411 where `getClipboardData:` calls `[UIPasteboard string]` synchronously on the main thread.

`UIPasteboard` operations can block when:

1. **Universal Clipboard** - Syncing clipboard content from other devices requires network operations
2. **Content type coercion** - Converting clipboard formats triggers inter-process communication
3. **Privacy checks** - iOS 14+ clipboard access permissions

Apple's `UIPasteboard` documentation doesn't guarantee these operations complete quickly, and blocking the main thread for 2+ seconds triggers iOS's "App Hang" detection.

## Expected results

Tapping on a TextField should not freeze the app.

## Actual results

The main thread blocks on `semaphore_wait_trap` for 2+ seconds while waiting for clipboard data, causing an "App Hang" (ANR).

## Minimal reproducible code sample

This issue is difficult to reproduce with a minimal sample because it depends on:

- Universal Clipboard sync timing between devices
- iOS system state regarding clipboard data availability/coercion

However, any app with a basic `TextField` can trigger this:

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(title: const Text('Clipboard Hang Repro')),
        body: const Padding(
          padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
          child: TextField(
            decoration: InputDecoration(
              hintText: 'Tap here while Universal Clipboard is syncing',
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

To reproduce:

1. Run on a physical iOS device with iCloud/Universal Clipboard enabled
2. Copy large content (e.g., an image or formatted text) on a paired Mac
3. Immediately tap the TextField before sync completes

comments: Keeping the issue open and labeling for team's tracking.

Thanks for filing this issue!

An easy fix might be to kick off a background task, read the pasteboard, switch back to the main thread, and then respond to the platform channel message ([1](https://embrace.io/blog/uipasteboard-security-and-performance/), [2](https://stackoverflow.com/a/43821700)). A better but more complex fix might be to switch to [`UIPasteboard.itemProviders`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uipasteboard/itemproviders) for reading the clipboard.

This issue leads to an interesting takeaway: to properly support Universal Clipboard, Flutter must have asynchronous clipboard APIs. While merged threads let us introduce synchronous clipboard APIs, these should not replace the existing async APIs.

> The root cause appears to be in FlutterPlatformPlugin.mm line 411 where getClipboardData: calls [UIPasteboard string] synchronously on the main thread.

IIRC `getClipboardData` is not the one used in text input's paste. The text input's blocking is likely happening here: 

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/7674e4682f8a9e5c2219414250c7c6e9f8bf09bb/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm#L1339

However, `getClipboardData` blocking the main thread is problematic in other places that use this API. So it should also be addressed.

I tried this out and measured the time on `hasStrings` call. - For local pastes, it typically takes less than 0.1ms. - For network calls, it can take a few ms. - For slow network calls, a system window shows up, indicating it's pasting from my mac: So I think iOS gets it covered and we should be good. @xavier-hunt did you see the progress window when pasting long content?

From the stack trace, it looks like you are using the old Flutter context menu: 
```
-[FlutterPlatformPlugin getClipboardData:]             (Flutter.framework) ← FlutterPlatformPlugin.mm:411
-[FlutterPlatformPlugin handleMethodCall:result:]      (Flutter.framework) ← FlutterPlatformPlugin.mm:145
__47-[FlutterEngine maybeSetupPlatformViewChannels]... (Flutter.framework) ← FlutterEngine.mm:674
__45-[FlutterMethodChannel setMethodCallHandler:]...   (Flutter.framework) ← FlutterChannels.mm:313
```
With latest Flutter, we use [system context menu](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/SystemContextMenu-class.html) by default, which doesn't call `-[FlutterPlatformPlugin getClipboardData:]` anymore, and it should provide the same UX as a native app.

@hellohuanlin sorry for the late response. Just checked Sentry, no events for past 24 days which more or less lines up with most recent Flutter upgrade.
Thank you to you and the team for the speedy response and follow ups.

@xavier-hunt thanks for the update. Since we already use system context menu by default, it would be lower priority to fix the old Flutter context menu. Marking it as P3 and un-asigning myself. CC @loic-sharma
This issue likely also affects `SelectionArea` and `SelectableText` which do not use the system context menu on iOS.
@Renzo-Olivares from triage: assigning to text input team for prioritization since you are looking into it. feel free to kick it back to team-ios.
The `fyi-text-input` label is redundant with the `team-text-input` label.

I was not able to reproduce this issue using `SelectableText` or `SelectionArea`. This makes sense if this issue is mainly centered around `getClipboardData` which they never call since you cannot paste into static text.

issues.title: `TextInputAction.next` in `Autocomplete` broken in Flutter 3.32.5 when inside a `Column`
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Flutter project.
2. Replace the contents of main.dart with the reproduction code below.
3. Run the app on Web plotform.
4. Tap into the Autocomplete field and type any character (e.g., "T") to trigger suggestions.
5. Select a suggestion by pressing `Enter ` key on keyboard.
6. Observe that focus does not move to the next TextFormField

### Expected results

When the user presses the `Enter `button on the keyboard after entering text or selecting an option in the Autocomplete field:

- Focus should move automatically to the next TextFormField below.
- This should work consistently when fields are arranged vertically using a Column.

This is standard behavior when using `TextInputAction.next` in Flutter input fields.

### Actual results

- Pressing the `Enter `key on the keyboard after interacting with the Autocomplete field does nothing.
- Focus remains on the Autocomplete field and does not move to the next TextFormField.
- No error is thrown, and no focus change occurs.

 This behavior works correctly if the same widgets are placed in a `Row`

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Padding(
          padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8.0),
          child: Column(
            children: [
              Autocomplete<String>(
                optionsBuilder: (TextEditingValue textEditingValue) {
                  return ["Checking", "Testing"];
                },
                onSelected: (String val){
                  print(val);
                },
                fieldViewBuilder: (context, controller, node, onFieldSubmitted){
                  return  TextFormField(
                    decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: "AutoComplete 1"),
                    textInputAction: TextInputAction.next,
                    controller: controller,
                    focusNode: node,
                    onFieldSubmitted: (String value) async {
                      onFieldSubmitted();
                    },
                  );
                },
              ),
              const SizedBox(height: 10,),
              TextFormField(
                textInputAction: TextInputAction.next,
                decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: "Form Field 1"),
              )
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```
